races_of_leonoria.general_description: Tall, slender, nimble, and magically gifted due to a natural affinity with norium in their bodies. They are often regarded as attractive, naturally charismatic, and elitist, viewing themselves as important custodians of wisdom[cite: 41, 42, 43].

sub_races.name: The Secluded
sub_races.description: Forest dwellers and isolationists with an aloof character. They are naturally connected to most magical entities and have a neutral affinity[cite: 44].
sub_races.habitat: Forested areas of The Sanctuary Lands[cite: 14, 17].

sub_races.name: The Greys
sub_races.description: City builders, scholars, and clerics with a Neutral-Good affinity. They tend toward religious fanaticism regarding logic and stoicism and possess strong convictions[cite: 46].

sub_races.name: The Dark Ones
sub_races.description: Secretive and unsocial beings gifted in dark arts and deceptive fighting. While naturally selfish, they can occasionally show a compassionate side and make effective leaders of darker forces[cite: 48, 49].

sub_races.name: The Ice Ancients
sub_races.description: Solitary, mystical, and extremely xenophobic. Their culture and background remain largely unknown[cite: 47].

races_of_leonoria.general_description: Human-like and the most plentiful race on Leonoria. While they lack extreme resistances, they are versatile but prone to easy corruption[cite: 50, 51, 52].

sub_races.name: Midlander
sub_races.description: Often referred to as 'commoners,' they are skilled traders, leaders, bards, and diplomats, though prone to religious fanaticism[cite: 50, 55].

sub_races.name: Northerner
sub_races.description: Isolationist hunters and traders who are good warriors with strong convictions regarding their faith[cite: 50, 54].

sub_races.name: Step Folk
sub_races.habitat: The Gleam Havens and The Blinding Lands[cite: 23, 26].

races_of_leonoria.general_description: Brutish, tall (up to 8 feet), and strong beings partially physically afflicted and maddened by The Great Death. They are naturally gifted melee warriors, resistant to cold, and have high resistance to moral afflictions[cite: 57, 58, 59, 60, 61].

sub_races.name: Foresters
sub_races.description: Hairy and mossy in appearance, providing natural camouflage. They are extreme isolationists with shamanistic faiths and a naturally neutral alignment[cite: 61, 62].
sub_races.habitat: The Dark Forests, The Blinding Lands, The Eternal Winds, and The Boglands[cite: 21, 26, 30, 37].

sub_races.name: Ravagers
sub_races.description: Large warrior types often serving as frontline mercenaries. They are known for cannibalism and a naturally evil alignment, though benign outliers exist[cite: 62, 63].
sub_races.habitat: The Dark Forests and other Wildmen territories[cite: 21, 37].

races_of_leonoria.general_description: Mutants whose destinies were shaped by the laws of nature being rewritten during The Great Death[cite: 64, 65].

sub_races.name: Oakpeople (Tree People)
sub_races.description: Small in stature and covered in leaf-like growths. They possess nature magic stronger than that of The Secluded and can commune with animals, though they struggle with communication and loyalty[cite: 66, 67, 68].
sub_races.habitat: Forested areas of The Sanctuary Lands[cite: 14, 17, 66].

sub_races.name: Stone Folk
sub_races.description: A collective term for what some argue are three different races. They live in varying levels of civilization[cite: 69, 70].
sub_races.habitat: Mountainous areas of The Sanctuary Lands and The Forgotten Kingdom (The Light and The Well zones)[cite: 16, 34, 69].

sub_races.name: Swampbrood
sub_races.description: A group of the elementally afflicted[cite: 64].
